frameworks.name: neural population coding of working memory
frameworks.one_line: visual features held in sustained spiking activity of feature-tuned populations

frameworks.name: drift in recurrent population activity
frameworks.one_line: random noise in recurrent connections causes memory representations to wander without losing spikes

frameworks.name: spiking-decay account of memory loss
frameworks.one_line: the same activity-reduction explains both set-size and retention-interval effects

frameworks.name: integrator / drift-diffusion model of decision making
frameworks.one_line: used to predict a fixed latency-precision coupling under the decay hypothesis

core_question: Does the precision loss of visual working memory over time reflect decay of population spiking activity or drift in that activity?
key_assumptions: visual features are stored in sustained population spiking
set-size effects arise from normalization of total spiking
an integrator decision model links spike rate to response latency and precision
manipulations: memory set size (1 to 4 colored discs)
key_findings: response latency increased with set size
recall precision decreased with longer retention delays
latency did not systematically change with retention delay
interpretations: retention-interval forgetting is driven by drift in population activity, not by decay of overall spiking
set-size and delay effects arise from distinct mechanisms within the population code
